prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, f⁰ and dual descriptor for pyridine; headline figure reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ (N atom).
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (RIJK) in PySCF 2.5.0.
• Geometry provenance: fixed single-point on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz (no re-optimization; therefore DFT//unknown).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1); N ± 1 states were treated as doublets (charge +1/–1, multiplicity 2) for the finite-difference Fukui evaluation.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF backend (functional “b3lyp”, basis “def2-tzvp”).
• Convergence & caveats: all three single-point SCFs (N, N+1, N–1) reported “converged: true”.
  Integrity checker verdict: status ok, trustworthy true.  Additional integrity notes (severity = warning):
  – "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – "common header present"
• Live log: the PySCF run was streamed during execution to a `.out` file created by the skill in the working directory (path echo displayed during the run).

Result (selected data)
– Most electrophilic site: atom 1 (N), f⁺ = 0.48422572
– Most nucleophilic site: atom 4 (C), f⁻ = 0.11826260
– Dual descriptor highlights N as the strongest electrophile and C4 as the strongest nucleophile (Δf = 0.42010305 and −0.23186359, respectively).

Headline numerical result (copied exactly): 0.48422572
